prompt: Draw the complete SN2 mechanism for the reaction of bromomethane with hydroxide ion. Show the curved arrow for the nucleophilic attack and the departure of the leaving group in a single concerted step.
hints: SN2 reactions occur in a single concerted step — no intermediates.
Draw a curved arrow from the lone pair on OH⁻ to the carbon bearing the Br.
Draw a second curved arrow from the C–Br bond toward Br, showing departure of bromide.

prompt: Draw the complete E2 elimination mechanism for 2-bromopropane reacting with sodium ethoxide. Show all curved arrows for proton abstraction and leaving group departure in the concerted step.
hints: E2 is a concerted, one-step mechanism requiring anti-periplanar geometry.
The base abstracts a β-hydrogen. Draw a curved arrow from the C–H bond to the base.
Show the π bond forming (C=C) and the Br departing simultaneously.
